content.text: Important catch before I proceed: the working tree already shows interference from your **other** Claude session — it had switched HEAD to `agent/entire-checkpoint-demo` and dropped an untracked `.entire/smoke-test.txt`. Both sessions share one checkout, so we're fighting over HEAD (exactly the concurrency gotcha, now live). I've moved to my own branch `agent/entire-checkpoint-test` and will only commit my own file. Let me create a small file with attributable lines.

input.content:
entire checkpoint smoke test (throwaway, opus session 9fae47dd)

line A: created to exercise entire's checkpoint capture
line B: entire blame should attribute these lines to this session
line C: entire why should trace back to the user's prompt

result.output: File created successfully at: /Users/elvis/Documents/ai/gtm-sdk/entire_smoketest_opus.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: Now commit only my file and observe the entire checkpoint machinery fire.

input.command:
cd /Users/elvis/Documents/ai/gtm-sdk
git add entire_smoketest_opus.txt
echo "=== committing (watch for entire hook output) ===" 
git commit -m "test: entire checkpoint smoke test" 2>&1
echo
echo "=== HEAD commit full message (trailer injected?) ===" 
git log -1 --format='%B'
echo "=== commit trailers only ==="
git log -1 --format='%(trailers)'
input.description: Commit test file and inspect for Entire-Checkpoint trailer
